title: Freshly Fried British Rye Toast
description: A smoky and crunchy British dish featuring rye, perfect for kids, with an ayurvedic-rajasic friendly twist. This recipe combines the earthy flavor of rye with a hint of fermentation.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
In a small bowl, mix together the sea salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
Butter one side of each rye bread slice.
Place the bread slices, buttered side down, in the skillet.
Sprinkle the salt, pepper, and caraway seed mixture evenly over the bread slices.
Cook for 4-5 minutes or until the bread is golden brown and crispy.
Flip the bread slices over and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the apple cider vinegar and water.
Brush the vinegar mixture over the bread slices.
Remove the bread from the skillet and let cool for a minute or two.
